The SAST scan identified 560 findings across crAPI. The overall risk score is 100/100, classified as Critical risk. Security grade: D. Policy status: FAIL.

Affected Components

Code Analysis Architecture

Visualization of scanned codebase modules, file distribution, and vulnerability hotspots.

Methodology & Scoring

Analysis Approach

Static Application Security Testing (SAST) analyzes source code, bytecode, and binary code for security vulnerabilities without executing the application. Pattern matching, taint analysis, and data-flow analysis identify potential weaknesses at the code level.

Severity Classification

  • Critical — CVSS 9.0–10.0
  • High — CVSS 7.0–8.9
  • Medium — CVSS 4.0–6.9
  • Low — CVSS 0.1–3.9

Risk Score Methodology

Risk score (0–100) is computed from weighted severity counts, exploit maturity, EPSS probability, affected surface area, and patch availability. Higher scores indicate greater risk.

Standards & References

  • MITRE CWE — Common Weakness Enumeration
  • OWASP Top 10 — Web Application Security Risks
  • CVSS v3.1 — Common Vulnerability Scoring System
  • EPSS — Exploit Prediction Scoring System

Prioritized Remediation Plan

# Action Item Description Effort Impact Findings
1 Remediate High Severity Findings Resolve 525 high severity findings within the current sprint. These are exploitable vulnerabilities with significant impact. 🏗 High High 525
2 Address Top CWE: CWE-306 The most frequently occurring weakness is CWE-306. Consider targeted developer training and code review focused on this category. 🛠 Medium High 0
3 Review Low Severity Findings Review 35 low severity findings and address as part of ongoing code quality improvements. ⚡ Low Low 35
